---
description: 'GPLv3 copyright header policy for all source files'
applyTo: '**/*.{py,sh}'
---

# GPLv3 Copyright Headers

## Policy

This project is licensed under **GPL-3.0-or-later**. Every `.py` and `.sh` file
**must** include the standard GPLv3 copyright header.

## Required Header

```text
# Copyright (C) 2026 Fabian Koerner <mail@fabiankoerner.com>
#
# This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify
# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or
# (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program.  If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
```

## Placement Rules

| Scenario           | Placement                                |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
| File has a shebang | Header goes **immediately after** line 1 |
| No shebang         | Header goes **at the very top**          |

A blank line separates the header from the file's content (docstring, imports, etc.).

## When Creating New Files

**Every new `.py` or `.sh` file must include the header.** After creating the file, run:

```bash
uv run scripts/add_gpl_headers.py <path-to-new-file>
```

The script is idempotent — running it on a file that already has the header is a no-op.

## Verification

To check that all tracked source files have the header:

```bash
uv run scripts/add_gpl_headers.py --check
```

To bulk-add headers to any files missing them:

```bash
uv run scripts/add_gpl_headers.py --all
```

## Exceptions

- `**/_version.py` — auto-generated by setuptools_scm, excluded automatically
- `docs/planning/legacy/` — third-party reference code, gitignored
- `py.typed` — PEP 561 marker file, conventionally empty
